q: Fetal development: Which sequence correctly describes human development from zygote to implantation?
options: Zygote → gastrula → morula → blastocyst → implantation in endometrium
Zygote → cleavage divisions (morula) → blastocyst (hollow ball with inner cell mass) → implantation in uterine endometrium
Zygote → blastocyst → morula → trophoblast → implantation in fallopian tube wall
Fertilized egg travels to uterus as a single cell, then divides after attaching to endometrium
explanation: After fertilization, the zygote undergoes cleavage to form a morula (solid ball), then cavitation produces a blastocyst (with trophoblast and inner cell mass); the blastocyst implants in the endometrium ~6-10 days post-fertilization.
